LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT DOUGLAS HENDRICKSON, CLIFF LABOY, INDEPENDENT SPORTS & ENTERTAINMENT INC. A DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED AS TO THE FIRST, SECOND, FOURTH, AND FIFTH CAUSES OF ACTION AND SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION. THE COURT STATES ITS REASONS AND FINDINGS IN OPEN COURT AND WITH ALL COUNSEL PRESENT, AS REFLECTED IN THE TRANSCRIPT OF THE CERTIFIED REPORTER AND IN THE CLERK'S .DOC (WORD) MINUTES. THE PREVAILING PARTY SHALL SUBMIT A [PROPOSED] WRITTEN ORDER THAT TRACKS THE COURT'S RULING, VERBATIM AND THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: ANGELA BRADSTREET; CLERK: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: MARIA VISCIO, CSR NO. 12353, EMAIL: MVISCIO@SFTC.ORG. (D302)

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Oct-11-2018 Vacated

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Dec-20-2017 Set For Trial On Nov-05-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses
Off Calendar Mar-06-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Susan Joo, Esq. of Hunton & Williams LLP, Tel. No. 415.975.3705, appearing for the Defendants;
No appearance by any other party or counsel.

Defense counsel stipulates that the matter may be heard by Judge Pro Tem Anne Hipshman.

There being no other appearance, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on Defendant Independent Sports & Entertainment Inc.'s Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses:

Defendants' Motion to Compel Further Interrogatory Responses is GRANTED as follows:

Form Interrogatory 2.5. Granted. Plaintiff is ordered to provide all residence addresses for the last five years. Defendants are entitled to receive this information and verify whether the addresses currently possess are complete.

Form Interrogatory 2.8. Granted. Plaintiff is ordered to respond to this Interrogatory by providing the information in his possession, custody or control in conformity with the categories contained in the form interrogatory regarding any felony convictions, without prejudice to any objections plaintiff may have in respect to the admissibility of this information in any future proceedings.

Special Interrogatory No. 2. Granted. Notwithstanding Defendants' use of "may," Plaintiff is ordered to respond to this Interrogatory to the extent he believes identified witnesses possess information relevant to his claims or defenses in this matter, and not information gathered by Plaintiff's counsel subject to work product. Order signed. Judge Pro Tem: Anne Hipshman, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Matter Not Reported. =(302/JPT/HEK)

Defendants Douglas Hendrickson, Cliff Laboy, Independent Sports & Entertainment Inc.'S Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint (For Tentative Ruling Only)
Off Calendar Aug-02-2017 Text Ruling

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-02-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Daniel S. Rubin of the law offices of Lessem, Newstat & Tooson, LLP (818-582-3087) appears via telephone as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorneys, Roland M. Juarez and Susan Joo, both of the law offices of Hunton & Williams (213-532-2145) appear as counsels for defendants/moving parties

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling with modification. The Court reads the ruling as modified into the record in open court and with both sides present.

DEFENDANT DOUGLAS HENDRICKSON, CLIFF LABOY, INDEPENDENT SPORTS & ENTERTAINMENT INC. A DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT is overruled as to the first, second, fourth, and fifth causes of action and sustained without leave to amend as to the third cause of action. The Court states its reasons and findings inopen court and with all counsel present, as reflected in the transcript of the certified reporter and in the Clerk's .doc (Word) minutes.

The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with CRC 3.1312.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-30-2017 Continued To Aug-30-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-10-2017 Sustained
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES - ATTY. DANIEL RUBIN OF THE LESSEM NEWSTAT & TOOSON, LLP, TEL. NO. 1-818-582-3087 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. SUSAN JOO OF THE HUNTON & WILLIAMS LLP, TEL. NO. 1-415-975-3705 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendants Douglas Hendrickson, Cliff LaBoy, and Independent Sports & Entertainment's demurrer to all five causes of action in the complaint filed by plaintiff Raymond Truitt is sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to all causes of action except the cause of action for conversion and is sustained without leave to amend as to the cause of action for conversion. The first cause of action for breach of contract fails as to the individual defendants because there is no allegation that either Mr. Hendrickson or Mr. LaBoy is a party to the alleged oral contract or a successor or an assignee of a party to the alleged contract. The breach of contract cause of action fails as to ISE because the alleged oral contract cannot be performed within a year and thus is presumptively barred by the statute of frauds and there are insufficient allegations of estoppel to assert the statute of frauds or of any other doctrine that makes the statute of frauds inapplicable or unenforceable. The second cause of action for conversion fails because there is no allegation that defendants converted a specific identifiable sum of money, nor is it reasonably possible that Mr. Truitt can make such an allegation in good faith. The third cause of action for unjust enrichment fails because Mr. Truitt does not allege - as an alternative to his allegation of an enforceable oral contract - that there is no enforceable contract. Without such an allegation, the unjust enrichment claim is duplicative of and cannot stand apart from the claim for breach of contract. "A plaintiff cannot pursue or recover on a quasi-contract claim if the parties have an enforceable agreement regarding a particular subject matter." (Klein v. Chevron U.S.A., Inc.(2012) 202 Cal.App.4th 1342, 1388-89.) The fourth cause of action for misappropriation of trade secrets fails because Mr. Truitt does not allege sufficient facts that his client list constitutes a "trade secret" or that defendants "misappropriated" the client list as those terms are used in the California trade secret statute. The fifth cause of action for fraud fails because Mr. Truitt does not adequately allege facts showing that any of the defendants made one or more representations to him, defendants knew that those representations were false and intended to induce reliance on the representations, and that he reasonably relied on those representations. Since it appears that it may be reasonably possible for Mr. Truitt to remedy the deficiencies in his claims except for his claim for conversion, if he can do so in good faith, he is given leave to do so. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen CSR #8189 =302/hk
